In this work, we develop a Vision-to-Force (V2F) pipeline that combines computer vision-based segmentation, active-contour refinement, and geometric feature extraction with a physics-informed residual neural network that augments a Hertz contact equation. The resulting model maps non-contact visual descriptors and cultivar metadata to predict a safe grasp force with mean validation performance of $R^2 \approx 0.7$ across unseen cultivar groups, which is a good result given the inherent mechanical variability of biological tissue. Experimental validation using a gripper and load cell indicates that the predicted forces enable stable manipulation of different types of date fruits, with residual deformations below 1 mm and no observable damage. These results show that pre-emptive, vision-driven force estimation% can replace slow and potentially damaging tactile exploration , enabling safer robotic handling of fragile fruits.

inputPapers.title: Defer to Plan: Adaptive Multi-Agent Fusion for End-to-End V2X Driving
inputPapers.abstract: Vehicle-to-everything-aided autonomous driving (V2X-AD) significantly enhances driving performance through information sharing. However, existing collaborative perception methods only optimize module-level perception capabilities and fail to effectively serve the ultimate planning and control tasks. We propose an end-to-end collaborative driving system that directly optimizes planning task performance. The system employs MotionNetwork to fuse historical temporal information, utilizes attention mechanisms to efficiently compress spatial features into compact tokens, and adaptively fuses multi-agent features through an autoregressive decoder. Additionally, we introduce Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture to enhance the model's representation capacity for heterogeneous features. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves a driving score of 79.72, surpassing the state-of-the-art CoDriving baseline (77.15) by 3.33% in closed-loop evaluation while maintaining communication efficiency.

inputPapers.title: EgoRecovery: Acquiring Failure Recovery Ability Through Human Recovery Demonstration
inputPapers.abstract: Robust embodied robots should be able to recover from failures and retry tasks in order to operate reliably in unstructured and noisy real-world environments. Achieving this capability requires training policies on data that captures recovery behaviors. However, collecting such data through robot teleoperation is difficult to scale, as it is time-consuming to induce diverse failure states, perform corrective actions, and reset the environment. This challenge is further exacerbated by the high diversity of failure modes, which demands substantially more recovery data than success demonstrations. In this work, we show that egocentric human data capturing failure recovery processes provides a scalable alternative. By efficiently arranging task-level failure configurations and recording short recovery segments, human operators can generate more than 10x as much valid recovery data per hour compared to robot teleoperation under our protocol. To address the embodiment gap between human and robot, we propose EgoRecovery, a co-training framework for learning recovery behavior, where human recovery demonstrations are aligned to a compact corrective-intent space shared with robot data, which captures the timing and magnitude of correction. Only a small number of robot recovery demonstrations are required to connect this intent to executable robot actions. At deployment, a learned recovery gate predicts when correction is needed from robot observations and activates the corrective intent only in recovery states. Experiments on real-world recovery tasks show that EgoRecovery improves success from failure starts over robot-only recovery, direct co-training with human recovery data, and direct intent-transfer baselines.

inputPapers.title: Koopman Dreamer: Spectrally Constrained Latent Dynamics for Stable World-Model Imagination
inputPapers.abstract: Latent world models improve sample efficiency in continuous control by optimizing policies over imagined latent trajectories, but common neural transitions offer limited direct control over modal persistence and error accumulation in long rollouts. We propose Koopman Dreamer, a Dreamer-style world model with a spectrally constrained deterministic latent dynamics core. Its Koopman-inspired backbone uses two-dimensional rotation--scaling blocks with bounded radii to represent damping, rotation, and near-periodic modes. Linear and low-rank bilinear action terms capture global and state-dependent control effects, while stochastic-state modulation supplies local correction information. To reduce the mismatch between posterior-conditioned training and prior-only imagination, the model combines posterior-conditioned EMA teacher targets with one-step consistency, multi-step rollout, and open-loop observation-prediction objectives. We further derive a multi-step rollout-error bound that separates amplification by the spectral backbone and bilinear interaction from the additive effects of stochastic-state mismatch and modeling residuals, clarifying the trade-off between error attenuation and long-term information retention. Experimental results on proprioceptive continuous-control tasks from the DeepMind Control Suite and UAV-LiDAR autonomous navigation demonstrate that Koopman Dreamer improves the stability of long-horizon latent rollouts and achieves stronger closed-loop control performance on tasks that rely on high-quality multi-step imagination.

inputPapers.title: Morphing MILR: Design and control of a cable-driven limbless robot with rolling joints for maneuvering in complex environments
inputPapers.abstract: Limbless robots offer exceptional mobility in confined and cluttered environments due to their slender bodies and their ability to exploit body-terrain interactions. Recent designs incorporating compliance demonstrate robust locomotion without complex sensing or control; however, these systems typically rely on fixed body configurations, with each morphology specialized for a single locomotion mode or environment. This raises a key challenge: how can a single limbless robot achieve versatile locomotion while preserving the robustness of compliance-mediated locomotion? To address this challenge, we present a cable-driven limbless robot that reconfigures body morphology and compliance to enable diverse locomotion modes. Distributed cable actuation generates traveling body waves, while programmable passive compliance enables robust contact-rich locomotion without terrain knowledge or high-bandwidth feedback. Rolling joints reorient bending planes along the body, enabling rapid reconfiguration and smooth transitions between locomotion styles, and incorporate geared locking to maintain configuration without continuous power. By combining programmable bending compliance and morphology control, the platform achieves lateral undulation, sidewinding, rolling, and twisting within a single system. Experiments demonstrate reliable gait generation, traversal in obstacle-rich environments, and transitions between modes, establishing a versatile limbless platform for navigating complex environments with applications in search and rescue, environmental monitoring, and inspection.

inputPapers.title: Contact-Persistent Full Actuation for Aerial Physical Interaction
inputPapers.abstract: Fully actuated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are usually certified through rank conditions on a control-allocation matrix or through free-flight tracking performance. For aerial physical interaction, this certification may be incomplete. During sustained contact, part of the available wrench is consumed by the interaction task, and only the residual wrench remains available for stabilization, disturbance rejection, and maneuvering. This paper introduces a control-theoretic framework for \emph{contact-persistent full actuation}. A rigid-body model on $\R^{3}\times\SO\left(3\right)$ is combined with a morphology-dependent wrench map that captures fixed-tilt, variable-tilt, coaxial, and overactuated multirotor architectures. We define feasible wrench sets under actuator limits, residual wrench sets under task loading, and residual authority margins that strengthen the usual rank-based notion of full actuation. The main result shows that contact-persistent full actuation is equivalent to interiority of the task wrench in the constrained feasible wrench polytope, and that the residual authority radius is exactly the distance to the polytope boundary. We further introduce a signed residual-margin certificate for infeasible and boundary cases, a slack-maximizing allocation certificate, and a robust implementability condition that can be used as a margin-aware safety filter. Numerical evaluation on an abstract tilted hexarotor shows that full row rank alone does not imply feasible contact operation. Intermediate tilt angles preserve residual authority during pushing, whereas small or excessive tilts fail because of lateral-force deficiency or hover-margin loss.

inputPapers.title: NavVerse: Benchmarking Indoor-to-Outdoor Embodied Navigation in Continuous Robot Simulation
inputPapers.abstract: Robots deployed in delivery, campus, and emergency-response settings often need to navigate from buildings to streets within a single continuous episode. Existing benchmarks usually evaluate indoor and outdoor navigation separately, and many abstract away robot execution, leaving exit finding, boundary traversal, adaptation, and kinodynamic failures underexplored. We introduce NavVerse, a physics-enabled benchmark for indoor-to-outdoor embodied navigation. NavVerse contains 100 indoor scenes, 50 urban outdoor scenes, and 50 indoor-to-outdoor scenes, and 10,000 episodes spanning Object Navigation, Vision-and-Language Navigation, and Place Navigation tasks, where agents search for semantic points of interest such as restaurants or banks. Agents are evaluated through executable robot interfaces using task-success, path-efficiency, and safety metrics. Zero-shot experiments with RL, VLA, and modular baselines show that current agents remain far from solving cross-context navigation: end-to-end VLAs obtain the highest zero-shot success, while the modular method provides the strongest safety profile. PlaceNav further reveals a clear drop from outdoor to indoor-to-outdoor scenes, indicating that adaptation remains major bottleneck.

inputPapers.title: Remote ID Spoofing-Aware Trajectory Planning for Small Unmanned Aerial Systems
inputPapers.abstract: This work presents a decentralized, spoofing-aware trajectory planning framework for small unmanned aerial systems operating under Remote Identification (RID) location spoofing attacks. Existing planners typically assume RID broadcasts are trustworthy, which can increase the risk of loss of separation and mid-air collisions when spoofing occurs. In contrast, the proposed approach explicitly treats RID information as unverified and incorporates physical-layer observations to assess broadcast credibility. Received signal-strength measurements from neighboring aircraft are used to detect spoofing and probabilistically localize a spoofing agent. The resulting uncertainty is converted into a risk-bounded unsafe region using a chance-constrained formulation and integrated into a per-agent Markov decision process-based planner. This enables real-time, decentralized collision avoidance while preserving mission objectives and scalability. Simulation results in a multi-aircraft package delivery scenario demonstrate reduced near mid-air collision events compared to planners that assume truthful RID data, while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-time execution.

inputPapers.title: LENS: LLM-guided Environment Simplification for Planning and Control in Clutter
inputPapers.abstract: Despite recent advances in general-purpose robotic manipulation, real-world multi-object clutter remains challenging to handle for today's prevalent approaches. The problem scales in complexity due to more objects and collisions, more unpredictable contact physics, distractors, and task ambiguity. Bridging this gap to real-world deployment requires effective scene abstractions; yet today, producing such abstractions requires extensive task-specific manual engineering, which does not scale. These abstractions are costly to generate and difficult to adjust or fine-tune. We instead propose a plug-and-play fix to automatically generate scene-specific, task-specific, adaptively updating abstractions on top of existing planning and control stacks. LLM-guided Environment Simplification (LENS) produces a de-cluttered abstracted scene representation by merging (e.g., stacked objects) or pruning (e.g., distant objects) scene entities in a closed loop in response to task progress. These dynamic, task-relevant abstractions are versatile and easy to use. In our experiments, we show that LENS improves classical planning, model-based control, and a vision-language-action model, across a diverse set of highly cluttered manipulation scenes. Project website: https://lens-2026.github.io/.

paper.title: Koopman Dreamer: Spectrally Constrained Latent Dynamics for Stable World-Model Imagination

rawOutput.titleZh: Koopman Dreamer: 用于稳定世界模型想象的谱约束潜在动态

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 在基于模型的强化学习中，智能体通过想象未来轨迹来提升样本效率，但传统神经网络建模的潜在动态在长时展开时容易积累误差，导致想象失真。现有方法缺乏对模态持久性（如周期运动、阻尼）的显式控制，使得长期预测不稳定，进而影响策略学习。本文关注如何在保持表达能力的同时，增强潜变量传播的稳定性。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文在连续控制任务上评估模型，输入为本体感知状态或LiDAR向量，输出为未来状态、奖励和动作的预测。模型采用Dreamer式架构，在隐空间进行序列想象，并优化策略。评价方式包括闭环控制性能、开环预测精度及谱半径敏感性分析，实验涵盖DeepMind Control Suite和UAV-LiDAR导航模拟环境。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出Koopman Dreamer，其核心是引入谱约束的确定性潜在动态结构，使模态衰减与振荡可通过学习的谱半径直接调控。实验显示该方法在9项任务中8项优于DreamerV3，UAV导航成功率从53.8%提升至73.8%，且开环预测误差显著降低，验证了结构化动态对长期想象的有效性。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 模型将确定性潜变量通过由二维旋转-缩放块构成的Koopman骨干网络传播，每个块的模态半径被限制在区间内，从而控制信息衰减速度。结合线性与低秩双线性动作项捕捉状态依赖控制效应，并利用后验调制信号注入局部修正信息。训练中采用EMA教师目标与多步回滚损失缓解前后验不匹配问题。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 当前验证基于仿真，物理部署需额外安全机制；最优谱范围需通过解码性能与闭环表现联合选择，而非越小越好。最值得阅读的是其将动力系统理论融入世界模型的设计思想、误差传播的理论界分析，以及关于稳定性与表达力权衡的实证结论，对长时序预测系统具有指导意义。

paper.title: DINS-IO: Learned Inertial Odometry via Differentiable INS Consistency

rawOutput.titleZh: DINS-IO: 基于可微惯性导航一致性学习的惯性里程计

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 现有基于神经网络的惯性里程计严重依赖高精度位置真值进行监督训练，这类数据需借助动捕、视觉惯性系统等昂贵设备获取，成本高且难以大规模采集。更关键的是，纯数据驱动的方式缺乏对惯性导航物理规律的建模，导致模型泛化能力差。本文旨在摆脱对密集标注数据的依赖，探索仅利用原始IMU信号本身的物理约束来训练高质量惯性里程计的可能性。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 模型输入为原始IMU数据流及姿态估计，输出为IMU频率下的密集体坐标系速度序列。训练分为两阶段：第一阶段完全无监督，利用惯性导航方程构建可微分的一致性损失；第二阶段使用少量带标签轨迹，通过低秩适配器直接监督预测的速度并校准其尺度。评估在TLIO和自采Tango数据集上进行，采用绝对与相对轨迹误差等标准指标衡量定位性能。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 论文提出DINS-IO框架，首次将strapdown惯性导航的速度递推关系转化为可微分的自监督损失，实现了无需位置标签的预训练。结合仅微调低秩适配器的高效微调策略，仅用少量标签即可完成尺度校准。实验表明，该方法在标准基准上达到甚至超过全监督模型的性能，验证了物理一致性作为强归纳偏置的有效性，显著降低了对标注数据的依赖。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心在于构建一个滑动窗口最小二乘系统，将预测速度经姿态旋转后与比力积分进行物理一致性校验，其残差构成可微损失。网络设计高频回归头以输出逐样本速度，满足该约束需求。由于物理方程无法确定速度的绝对尺度，故引入第二阶段：冻结主干网络，仅用少量标签监督预测速度，并通过LoRA微调回归头，既完成尺度校准又避免破坏已学得的物理一致特征。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 方法依赖较准确的姿态估计（AHRS），在极端动态或磁干扰下可能退化。此外，共享加速度计偏置假设在长时间运行中可能不成立。尽管如此，论文的价值在于提出了‘物理规律即监督信号’的新范式。最值得阅读的是其方法设计部分，清晰展示了如何将经典导航方程融入深度学习训练，为低成本、高泛化惯性感知提供了新思路。

summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心在于构建一个滑动窗口最小二乘系统，将预测速度经姿态旋转后与比力积分进行物理一致性校验，其残差构成可微损失。网络设计高频回归头以输出逐样本速度，满足该约束需求。由于物理方程无法确定速度的绝对尺度，故引入第二阶段：冻结主干网络，仅用少量标签监督预测速度，并通过LoRA微调回归头，既完成尺度校准又避免破坏已学得的物理一致特征。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 方法依赖较准确的姿态估计（AHRS），在极端动态或磁干扰下可能退化。此外，共享加速度计偏置假设在长时间运行中可能不成立。尽管如此，论文的价值在于提出了‘物理规律即监督信号’的新范式。最值得阅读的是其方法设计部分，清晰展示了如何将经典导航方程融入深度学习训练，为低成本、高泛化惯性感知提供了新思路。

paper.title: KineBench: Benchmarking Embodied World Models via IDM-Free Kinematic Grounding

rawOutput.titleZh: KineBench：通过无逆动力学模型的运动学锚定来评测具身世界模型

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 当前评测具身世界模型（EWMs）面临一个核心难题：如何区分模型生成的视频本身违反物理规律，还是动作提取过程出错。现有方法依赖逆动力学模型（IDM）从像素序列中恢复动作，但这类模型在面对新物体或新场景时泛化能力差，导致评估结果存在归因模糊。这使得难以判断失败源于世界模型缺陷还是IDM误差，阻碍了对模型真实物理合理性的可靠衡量。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文构建了一个名为KineBench的新评测体系，在ManiSkill3环境中设计了20个多样化操作任务，并分为四个递进的测试套件：基础执行、任务迁移、视觉分布外泛化和复杂度条件下的扩展性分析。输入是语言指令和初始观测图像，输出是由世界模型生成的视频，系统通过视觉模型直接提取末端执行器的6D姿态并送入物理仿真器进行闭环执行，以任务成功率为核心指标。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: KineBench首次实现了无需IDM的闭环评测，显著降低了动作提取带来的归因歧义。实验发现，更大的模型在简单任务上表现更好，但在接触密集任务中仍存在挑战；同时揭示了任务复杂度依赖的非线性扩展规律——随着任务变难，增加数据和算力的边际收益递减，这对未来EWM的训练策略提供了实证指导。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 其核心是显式的运动学锚定管道：利用YOLO分割末端执行器区域，结合MoGeV2估计单目深度图，再通过FoundationPose基于CAD模型进行6D姿态匹配。该流程不依赖端到端学习的动作映射，而是通过几何约束过滤掉微小的像素抖动，同时对抓取器消失或空间跳跃等严重物理幻觉保持敏感，从而提供更稳定可靠的闭环接口。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 尽管提升了稳定性，该方法仍受限于分割与深度估计的质量，且当前轨迹评估依赖仿真环境中的专家路径。最值得阅读的是其问题洞察——指出IDM带来的系统性评估偏差，以及整个系统设计如何通过模块化解耦提升可诊断性。对于关注生成模型物理合理性、机器人仿真与现实差距、或评测可信度的研究者极具参考价值。

paper.title: Contact-Persistent Full Actuation for Aerial Physical Interaction

rawOutput.titleZh: 用于空中物理交互的接触持续全驱动

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 传统多旋翼无人机在自由飞行中表现良好，但在与环境持续接触时面临挑战，因为其姿态和力耦合。现有认证方法依赖于控制分配矩阵的秩条件或轨迹跟踪性能，无法确保接触过程中仍具备足够的剩余力矩进行稳定和扰动抑制。本文指出，即使系统满秩，若任务力接近可行域边界，也可能因执行器饱和而失效，因此需要一种能评估接触持久性的新框架。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 论文研究了一类刚体建模于R3×SO(3)上的全驱动无人机，涵盖固定倾斜、可变倾转、同轴及冗余驱动架构。输入为机体坐标系下的期望接触任务力矩（如推力），输出为各推进模块的推力分配。通过形态相关的力矩映射建立分配矩阵，并考虑执行器上下限。评价核心是任务力是否位于可行力矩集内部及其剩余稳定能力半径，而非单纯可行性或跟踪精度。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 本文提出了‘接触持续全驱动’的概念，定义为任务力矩下仍存在非零六维残差力矩空间以支持闭环稳定。关键贡献在于将此性质等价于任务力处于可行集内部，并证明最大残差半径即为到集合边界的距离。数值实验显示，满秩六的倾斜六旋翼在小或大倾角下仍会丧失推力能力，而中等倾角可最大化残差权威，验证了仅靠秩条件不足以保障接触操作。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 方法基于刚体力学与凸几何，构建受执行器限制的可行力矩多面体，并引入残差力矩集与残差权威半径。利用多面体的半空间表示计算带符号的裕度，从而判断任务是否可行且留有稳定余量。进一步提出基于分配松弛的证书与鲁棒可实现性条件，使该框架可用于实时安全滤波，确保指令始终保留所需稳定能力。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 该框架目前为静态分析，未考虑变量形变过程中的动态响应与伺服速率限制，也假设精确建模。结论适用于设计阶段评估形态合理性，但实际闭环性能还需结合控制器设计。最值得读的是其问题设定与理论框架，它重新定义了‘全驱动’的意义，强调约束下内部性比结构性秩更重要，对开发真正用于物理交互的无人机具有指导价值。

paper.title: Courteous Anticipation: Improving Long-Lived Task Planning in Persistent Shared Environments

rawOutput.titleZh: 礼貌性预期：改进持久共享环境中的长寿命任务规划

summarySections.backgroundAndQuestion: 在多个机器人长期共存的环境中，传统任务规划方法只关注当前任务的完成效率，忽视其行为对未来任务的影响，导致终端状态产生负面副作用，增加后续任务的整体成本。尤其当机器人能力与职责不同时，这种短视行为会显著加剧协作摩擦。论文旨在解决缺乏前瞻性与协作意识所引发的累积代价上升问题。
summarySections.modelAndSetup: 研究设定为多个机器人依次接收任务，在持续存在的环境中执行操作，前一任务的结束状态即为下一任务的初始状态。输入是当前任务与环境状态，输出是动作序列。通过PDDL建模任务与动作，采用FastDownward求解候选计划，并基于图神经网络估计各机器人未来任务成本，最终选择综合即时与预期代价最小的方案。
summarySections.contributionsAndResults: 提出‘礼貌性预期’规划机制，使机器人在完成自身任务时主动考虑对其他机器人未来任务的影响。在家庭与餐厅两个模拟环境中，相比短视规划和自私预期规划，总任务成本分别降低10.43%和17.41%。结果表明，显式建模跨智能体影响能显著提升长期协作效率，尤其在能力异构场景下增益更明显。
summarySections.methodsAndTechniques: 核心方法是将联合未来成本分解为各机器人独立的预期成本估计，每个机器人拥有一个基于图神经网络的评估器，预测从当前状态开始完成其后续任务的成本。规划时聚合所有评估器输出，指导选择对全局更友好的计划。该分解避免了组合爆炸式的联合 rollout，支持模块化扩展，新增机器人只需训练其独立评估器。
summarySections.limitationsAndReadingValue: 方法假设任务顺序到达且环境状态不变，未处理并发执行或多智能体动态干扰。此外，评估依赖于离线训练的估计器，泛化性受限于训练分布。尽管如此，论文的问题设定与系统设计极具启发性，最值得阅读的是其对‘礼貌性’的机制化定义、模块化成本估计架构及在异构多机器人场景中的实证分析。
